title_en: Get an Exclusive Sneak Peek of Archie #1, As Oni Press and Archie Comics Craft a Bold, Ambitious Reboot of a Pop Culture Icon
summary_en: Archie Comic and Oni Press are joining forces for an ambitious reboot of the Archie franchise. Just how big a change is this? Find out in our exclusive preview.
body_en:
Archie and the Riverdale crew are gearing up for what may well be their most ambitious reboot ever. In case you missed the news, Archie Comics and Oni Press have joined forces for a new line of books that will push the long-running franchise in strange and wonderful directions. Naturally, that includes the flagship Archie series, which will be helmed by writer Ben H. Winters (EC's Cruel Universe) and artist Fábio Moon (Daytripper).
With Comic-Con looming and the Archie relaunch drawing near, IGN can exclusively debut a new preview of Archie #1. Get a closer look in the slideshow gallery below:
Archie #1 features a main cover by Stuart Immonen (All-New X-Men, New Avengers) and variant covers by Moon, Karl Kerschl (Adventures of Superman), Khary Randolph (Starborn), Travis Charest (WildC.A.T.s), Kano (Gotham Central), Dan Parent (Archie), Elsa Charretier (Love Everlasting), and Mahmud Asrar (Conan the Barbarian).
Here's the official summary for Archie #1:
“I gotta say, writing Archie comics is pure joy... just like reading them,” said Winters in a statement. “There is something so simple and powerful about this set of friends, their loyalty to and love for one another, the way their comic misadventures reflect an exaggerated version of the traumas, dramas, and hijinks familiar to anyone who's ever been a teenager.”
Archie #1 will be released on September 2. That launch will be followed by Sabrina the Teenage Witch #1 in October and Archie in Hell #1 in November. You can preorder all three books at your local comic shop .
For more, learn about the big changes unfolding at Marvel Comics and see our exclusive preview of Sonic the Hedgehog x Godzilla #2 .
Jesse is a mild-mannered staff writer for IGN. Allow him to lend a machete to your intellectual thicket by following @jschedeen on BlueSky .
